fix(prompts): never flag a mismatch against a blank language side (#831)

toLanguage() defaults a blank/missing language to 'en' for prompting, which
masqueraded as a genuine detection and let a known non-en side (e.g. "de")
falsely read as a mismatch against a blank side. Gate mismatch on the raw
pre-normalization blank check instead, and update the stale test that
encoded the old buggy expectation.

fix(scraping): cap the personio feed body read (#830)

try_personio fetches the Personio XML feed through the SSRF-guarded
get_guarded client, but read the body with an unbounded res.text().await.
PR #821 capped the sibling generic-HTML fallback in the same resolver at
8 MB via read_text_capped; apply the identical fix here, returning
Ok(None) on an over-cap/error body the same way the redirect-follow path
already does.

fix: small advisory batch (rust) (#829)

* perf(autopilot): skip duplicate note candidates within one run

run_notes_loop keyed only against prior runs, so the same NEW job surfaced
under two URL variants in one run bought an AI note for each; the later
merge_found_jobs collapsed them and discarded one - waste against the
ASSISTANT_NOTES_MAX ceiling. Track canonical keys seen this run and skip the
second variant (before charge_daily, so it also spares the shared ceiling).

The oversized autopilot_helpers inline test module is moved to a sibling
tests.rs so mod.rs stays under the R8 LOC cap; no test behavior changes.

Source: #813 follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ai-generations): enforce one aggregate row per job url with upsert

save_application released the store lock between find_by_job_url and the
insert, so two concurrent saves for one job could both miss and both insert,
forking the per-job aggregate. Add a UNIQUE(job_url) partial index (WHERE
job_url != '', so deliberately unlinked '' rows still coexist) and recover
from the resulting insert conflict by merging into the row the other writer
created. The migration collapses any pre-existing fork before creating the
index, keeping the linked-or-newest row.

Two applications delete/detach tests inserted duplicate-url child rows to
exercise multi-row cleanup; they now use distinct urls per the new index.

Source: #816 follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The set-status contention test asserted events.len() >= ITERS; tighten it to
the exact count (ITERS * 2 transitions across the two threads + 1 creation
seed event). Also drop the stale tail of set_status's comment claiming
.transaction() needs a &mut *guard - the code uses guard.transaction() via
auto-ref.

Source: #811 nits.

fix: persist completed stream text so the poll fallback works (#828)

* fix(ai): persist the completed stream text for the poll fallback

The renderer's poll fallback (awaitAiStream) recovers a dropped stream frame or a
missed `done` event by reading `result.text` from the completed job, but the backend
persisted only `{ done: true }` — so PR #802's longer-wins fix was a runtime no-op.

Accumulate the completed answer (non-thinking deltas only) in the provider-agnostic
streaming completion path (`stream::finish`, shared by openai/anthropic/gemini/ollama)
and in the CLI-agent path (`cli_agent::emit_done`), and persist it as `result.text`.
Both strip inline `<think>…</think>` via a shared `strip_think_blocks` mirroring the
renderer's think-splitter, so the persisted text is the SAME shape the renderer buffers:
the poll's `persisted.length > buffer.length` compares like-for-like and can never
resolve reasoning markup into the final document.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(generate): align the stream poll test with the real job contract

The prior mock returned `{ status: 'completed', result: { text } }`, a shape the
backend never produced, so the regression test proved nothing. Mirror the real
contract (`result: { done: true, text }`, text think-stripped) and add a case pinning
that a recovered persisted result can never surface `<think>` markup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

A blur → refocus-within-150ms → blur burst overwrote `blurTimerRef.current`

fix: pr-batch advisory follow-ups (extension) (#825)

* fix(extension): let a hidden resume file input count as a decisive field

A custom upload widget hides the native <input type=file> (display:none)
behind a styled button, which defeated the visible-field short-circuit in
looksLikeApplicationForm: a real application form with a hidden resume input
and fewer than three other visible fields went unrecognized (#786 follow-up).

A resume/CV-flavored file input (name/id/label or a document `accept`) is now
decisive even when hidden, checked across all inputs; the narrowing to
resume-flavored inputs keeps the module under-reporting rather than over-
reporting. Also close the two #786 lows: a "Save draft"/"Add another"
type=submit button is skipped via the SubmitEvent's submitter, and
checkbox/radio-only forms no longer count as application forms.

* fix(extension): cover camelcase compounds in the identity key patterns

textSignal lowercases without splitting camelCase, so name="workCity"
flattens to "workcity" and no longer matched the location pattern because
"work" was not an enumerated city/town prefix (#784 follow-up). Add "work"
to the enumerated prefix so workCity resolves to location, while keeping the
leading-anchor enumeration so "ethnicity" stays a non-match (no new mis-fill
surface). Phone needs no change: "work" is already enumerated, so workPhone
matches, and headphone/smartphone correctly do not.

Pin the behavior: headphone in the phone non-match set, workphone in the
phone match set, and workcity/homecity in the location match set.

fix(agent): clamp confirm-request args to the called tool's own cap (#823)

ARGS_DISPLAY_CAP is the max of COVER_LETTER_CAP (20k) and SAVED_RESUME_CAP
(40k), so both gated Write tools displayed up to 40k. save_cover_letter then
truncates at its own 20k cap, so a 20k-40k letter was shown and edited in full
and silently clipped on save — the user approved text that was not the text
persisted.

Taking the max guarantees the "never show LESS than is saved" half of the
module's display/edit-fidelity invariant; it does not hold the "never show more"
half for whichever tool has the smaller cap.

Add display_cap_for(tool) and clamp with it. ARGS_DISPLAY_CAP stays as the
fallback for an unknown/future tool, preserving the never-show-less guarantee by
default. The module's security-invariant doc is updated to match.

Needs a >20k-char generated letter to bite, so this is about keeping the gate's
guarantee exact rather than a failure seen in practice.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(scraping): cap the body read on the user-supplied-url path (#821)

resolve_uncached called Response::text() on the generic-HTML fallback, buffering
the whole body with no size limit. That URL is attacker-influenced — which is
why the fetch goes through get_guarded_following_redirects — so a hostile or
broken host could stream an arbitrarily large body. The 20s timeout bounds how
long we read, not how much.

The board path already caps at 8 MB inside fetch_text, with a Content-Length
pre-check and a streamed accumulation that aborts mid-body. fetch_text is not a
drop-in here: it uses the shared client and would drop the SSRF guard.

Extract that logic into scraping::http::read_text_capped(response, cap) — same
charset handling, so umlauts still decode — have fetch_text call it so there is
one implementation, and read the scrape_url fallback through it at 8 MB.

Adds wiremock tests for both directions of the helper; the 40 existing
scraping::http tests cover the fetch_text refactor.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(credentials): never treat an unreadable metadata index as empty (#819)

load_meta mapped BOTH a read error and a parse error to unwrap_or_default() and
cached the result. Since the writer persists the whole map, the next set/remove
then wrote a one-entry file and orphaned every previously saved credential from
the index — the keychain secrets survive, but nothing can find them again.

Separately, set/remove took the cache lock inside load_meta, released it, and
retook it inside save_meta, so two concurrent writers could start from the same
snapshot and the second would drop the first's entry.

read_meta_file now separates "file absent" (empty index, first run) from
"unreadable/corrupt" (an error). load_meta still degrades to empty for read-only
callers but no longer caches that, and mutate_meta holds the lock across the
whole read-modify-write and refuses to write over an index it could not read,
leaving the user's file recoverable. save_meta is subsumed by mutate_meta.

Adds a corrupt-index test (asserting the file is left byte-for-byte intact) and
an 80-write two-thread concurrency test.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ai-generations): key the per-job aggregate on the normalized url (#816)

save_application looked its row up with the raw job_url, while the sibling
ApplicationStore dedupes on normalize_job_url. So the same job reached under
different tracking params — the norm on query-id boards like Indeed — missed its
own aggregate and forked a second "one-per-job" row, scattering the resume,
cover letter and answers for one job across two records.

The mismatch is already documented in commands/ai_generations.rs:84-86 ("which
never matches for query-id boards like Indeed").

Normalize on the write path so the stored key matches ApplicationStore's, and
fall back to a raw-url lookup so a row written before this change still merges
and is migrated onto the normalized key instead of being orphaned. The fallback
is guarded so an unusable url (normalize -> "") cannot merge into an unrelated
legacy row.

AiGenerationStore::applied_job_urls has no production consumer (the applied flag
comes from ApplicationStore's, already normalized), so nothing downstream
changes shape.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(autopilot): key the ai-notes skip on the merge's canonical key (#813)

The skip set was built from raw job URLs, but merge_found_jobs collapses jobs by
merge_key -> canonical_job_key -> normalize_job_url, which strips tracking params
and fragments. So a job re-surfacing as ...?utm_source=indeed was "new" to the
skip and "already known" to the merge: a Completer::complete call was made and
charged, then its note was discarded.

Aggregators append varying utm_* params, so this fired on essentially every run
rather than never — the opposite of the intent stated right above the check
("in steady state (nothing new) this makes ZERO provider calls").

Carry HashSet<String> of canonical keys through generate_assistant_notes into
run_notes_loop and compare on that.

The existing zero-calls test is renamed and strengthened: the already-seen jobs
now re-surface WITH tracking params, which is the case that was broken. With the
comparison reverted to the raw URL it fails 2 calls vs 0 expected.

autopilot_helpers/mod.rs is 1392 LOC on main against R8's 1400 cap; the first
draft hit 1403 and R8 caught it locally, so this lands at 1399.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(applications): read the row inside set_status's transaction (#811)

set_status called self.get(id), which takes and RELEASES the mutex, and only
then opened the write transaction. A transition committing in that gap was
invisible to the call, so the status_events row it appended recorded a
from_status the row no longer had — an append-only history containing a link
that never happened.

The transaction already existed so the UPDATE and its event row land together;
the read was just left outside it. Move it in via row_by_id_conn.

This keeps set_status an unconditional setter — transition_status_if remains
the compare-and-set path, and its doc comment already warns about exactly this
get-then-set pattern.

The new race test walks status_events by rowid (commit order; the `at` column
is millisecond-resolution and ties freely) and asserts each from_status equals
the previous to_status. It fails 3/3 against the old code and passed 5/5 with
the fix. Net +2 LOC, keeping the file under the R8 cap.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(prompts): only flag a language mismatch when both languages are known (#809)

validateMetadata computed mismatch as
  (parsed.resumeLanguage ?? 'en') !== (parsed.jobAdLanguage ?? 'en')

which is wrong twice: there is no 'unknown' guard, so an undetected side reads
as a mismatch, and ?? is nullish-only, so the empty string the model often
returns survives as '' and compares unequal to 'en'.

Both siblings already require BOTH sides to be known — analyze/validate.ts:126
and @ajh/shared detectLanguages:147 — and validateMetadata never got the guard.
A false mismatch injects a "different languages, rewrite entirely, do not
translate" instruction into the generation prompt.

Coerce the two languages first (blank falls back to 'en', as a missing key
already did), then apply the both-known rule. targetLanguage uses the coerced
jobAdLanguage for the same reason.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(monitoring): gate the running pulse on a stable id, not a translated label (#807)

animate was `label === 'Running'`, but label is t('monitoring.metrics.running').
That literal only matches when the active translation happens to be "Running",
so on any other locale — de renders "Läuft" — the card never pulsed while jobs
were running.

Give each metric a stable id and gate on id === 'running'. The list key moves
from the translated label to the id for the same reason.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(extension): anchor the collision-prone denylist terms (#805)

referr, reference and search were plain-substring entries in AMBIGUOUS, so
signal.includes() fired inside ordinary words: referr in "preferred", search in
"research", reference in "preferences". That skipped "Preferred first name",
"Preferred pronouns", "Research experience" and "Work preferences".

isAmbiguousSignal gates BOTH consumers — autofill's isCandidateField and
answers-capture's isCapturable — so those fields were neither filled nor
captured, and the user's typed answer was never learned.

Move the three into a leading-anchored AMBIGUOUS_PREFIXED regex. Only the
leading side is anchored: _ is a word character, so \b would stop matching
job_search / reference_name, and the trailing side must stay open for referral,
referrer, references and searchTerm. Same shape as the identity-keyword fix in
#784. referral drops out as redundant with referr.

Other substring hits in the list (company in "accompany", parent in "apparent",
tax in "syntax") are left alone — none is a plausible application-form label.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(generate): prefer the persisted result over a truncated stream buffer (#802)

The job-status poll resolved buffer || job.result?.text, consulting the
persisted text only when the buffer was EMPTY. That covers a lost final done
chunk, but not a lost interior delta — which leaves a truncated but truthy
prefix that short-circuits the ||, gets resolved as the finished document and is
then persisted by the caller.

The poll exists because deltas can be dropped, so the buffer is exactly the
value that cannot be assumed complete. Resolve whichever of the two is longer:
a partial stream can never beat the persisted result, and a missing result.text
still falls back to what did stream.

Same principle the timeout path already states — never persist truncated output
as success.

Adds stream-promise.test.ts; the truncated-prefix case fails on the old code.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Saeed Kolivand <51081940+saeedkolivand@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(monitoring): give the last-24-hours sparkline an actual 24-hour window (#800)

The chart is labelled "Last 24 hours" but had no time filter, and
new Date(ts).getHours() bucketed every completion the queue has ever held into a
0-23 hour-OF-DAY slot. A job finished at 3pm three days ago therefore shared a
bar with one finished at 3pm today, and nothing bounded allJobs.

Move the binning into a tested pure helper that filters to the window and
buckets by hours-ago, oldest bucket first, so the bars read left to right
chronologically and match the label. now is a parameter, so the caller owns the
clock. The finishedAt ?? updatedAt choice and the completed-only filter are
unchanged.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Saeed Kolivand <51081940+saeedkolivand@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(monitoring): dedup the activity feed on the real job id (#798)

The live/historical merge built its dedup set from a.id.split('-')[0]. A job id
is itself hyphenated (format!("job-{}", Uuid::new_v4())) and a live item's id is
`${event.jobId}-${event.ts}`, so that expression was ALWAYS the literal "job":
the set held one useless entry, no historical id matched it, the filter removed
nothing, and every completed/failed/cancelled job rendered twice. The two rows
carry different React keys, so nothing else surfaced it.

Strip only the trailing -<ts> to recover the job id.

Adds useActivityFeed.test.ts covering both the dedup and the non-duplicate case;
both fail on the old code.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(jobs): mark a posting applied only after tailor succeeds (#796)

handleTailor called trackInteraction('applied') before awaiting
saveFromPosting, and both failure paths returned without reverting it.
trackInteraction persists through persistJobMutation, so a Tailor that visibly
failed still left the posting recorded as Applied — badge and stored
interaction — contradicting the error toast shown at the same moment.

Move the call below the res?.id guard, matching the sibling handleSave, which
already marks 'bookmarked' only after the mutation resolves.

Adds tests for the reject and missing-id paths; both fail on the old code.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(autopilot): derive step patches from the reducer state, not a snapshot (#794)

handleStep built its runStates/stepLogs patches from the render snapshot it
closed over (deps [runStates, stepLogs]). Both reducers are pure
{ ...prev, ...patch } merges, so the value is fixed before dispatch: two step
events for the same autopilotId inside one React batch both read the same base
and the second overwrote the first — a dropped step-log line and a skipped
machine transition.

Let the reducers take a functional patch and compute both from prev. The
callback no longer closes over state, so its deps drop to [] and its identity
is stable. Plain object patches at the other call sites are unchanged.

Regression test fails on the old code with
  expected [ 'scrape_done' ] to deeply equal [ 'scrape_start', 'scrape_done' ]

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(prompts): stop the italic strip from eating bold markup (#792)

extractPlainText's italic pass, /\*([^*]+)\*/g, is not bold-aware. Inside
**bold** the leftmost match is the inner *bold*, so bold was silently
downgraded — and because [^*]+ also matches spaces and commas, two adjacent
bold spans paired up ACROSS each other:

  "Skills: **Python**, **Go**, **Kubernetes**" -> "Skills: *Python, Go, Kubernetes*"

The prompts instruct the model to emit 2-3 **keyword** bolds per bullet and
this runs on every generated résumé, cover letter, email and answer, so it
corrupted essentially every document.

Guard both delimiters against an adjacent asterisk. Genuine single-* italics
still strip; *** still collapses to ** (which is what the preceding pass says
it is for).

The one existing assertion for ***strong*** -> *strong* documented the
downgrade rather than intending it; it now expects **strong**.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shared): map the iso codes franc actually emits for zh, ar and no (#790)

LANGUAGE_MAP keyed Chinese/Arabic/Norwegian on the macrolanguage ISO 639-3
codes zho/ara/nor. franc emits the INDIVIDUAL code — cmn, arb, nob/nno —
so those three entries were dead and detectLanguage fell through to 'unknown'
for every résumé or job ad in those languages.

Verified against the installed franc 6.2.0: Chinese -> cmn, Arabic -> arb,
Norwegian -> nob.

Consequences were silent: detectLanguages(...).mismatch needs both sides known,
so the different-language warning never fired for those users, and referral
drafts collapse 'unknown' to 'en', handing a Chinese or Arabic user an English
draft.

Same class as the swe -> sv fix in #679. The existing tests only covered
en/de/fr, whose codes franc does emit, so nothing caught it; this adds real
text in each of the three scripts.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(extension): reply via the response callback so popup requests work on chrome (#788)

The onMessage listener returned handleRequest(...) — a Promise. That is the
Firefox browser.* convention; Chromium's chrome.runtime.onMessage requires
sendResponse plus a LITERAL true return to keep the channel open. A Promise is
truthy but not true, so Chrome closed the channel with no reply, sendMessage
resolved undefined, and popup.ts reported "No response from the extension
background." for every request/response action.

@wxt-dev/browser does not bridge that: its whole runtime is
  export const browser = globalThis.browser?.runtime?.id ? globalThis.browser : globalThis.chrome;

fix(extension): only auto-track a submit that came from the application form (#786)

The capture-phase submit listener fired for EVERY form on the page and posted
nothing but location.href, so pressing Enter in the site's search box (or
submitting a newsletter signup) auto-advanced a saved application to applied.
Separately, APPLY_TEXT_RE's unanchored 'apply' matched a bare "Apply now"
role=button — on a listing page that is the control that OPENS the application,
not the one that sends it.

Gate the submit path on looksLikeApplicationForm (a visible file input, or >= 3
visible fillable fields — a search box, newsletter signup and login form all
fall below that). Keep the broad text pattern for a control inside such a form,
where the structure corroborates it, and require an explicit send verb for a
control with no form around it, which is the pure-SPA case.

Known remaining gap, pre-existing: a "Save draft" submit button inside the real
application form still submits that form and so still fires. Telling those apart
needs SubmitEvent.submitter rather than the form.

fix(extension): anchor the city and phone identity keywords (#784)

city|town|phone|mobile were unanchored substrings in NAMED_KEY_PATTERNS, so
'city' matched "ethnicity", 'phone' matched "smartphone"/"iphone" and 'mobile'
matched "automobile". An EEO Ethnicity field was filled with the user's city and
a "Smartphone model" field with their phone number — a mis-fill, which this
module's contract explicitly forbids ("under-fills rather than mis-fills").

The neighbouring handy/mobil/ort/ville terms are already \b-anchored with a
comment naming this exact hazard; these four were simply left bare.

Anchor only the LEADING side via (?:^|[^a-z]): _ is a regex word character, so a
\b/trailing anchor would stop matching phone_number, candidate_phone,
mobile_number and city_1 — and textSignal does not split camelCase, so
phoneNumber arrives as "phonenumber". Legitimate compounds (cell/home/work/day/
tele + phone, hometown) are spelled out.

Adds field-signal.test.ts covering both directions.

fix(applications): reject a bad-typed next_action_at instead of clearing it (#782)

The handler mapped nextActionAt through Value::as_u64(), which returns None for
a negative, fractional, oversized, string or object value. That None is the same
Some(None) the store reads as "clear the reminder", so a caller trying to SET a
bad-typed reminder silently CLEARED it and the call still reported success.

Every other bad input on this handler is rejected — validate_recipient_email and
reject_oversized_job_description both return AppError::Validation — so do the
same here, via a pure parse_next_action_at helper shaped like its sibling.

Rust-only: the Zod schema (z.number().int().nullable().optional(), no .min(0))
could also be tightened, but that runs through the IPC codegen. The server-side
guard is the real trust boundary regardless, since a direct IPC caller bypasses
the renderer's validation.

fix(spend): recognise a port-less ipv6 loopback as local (#780)

is_localhost_url stripped the port via the LAST colon. An IPv6 host has colons
of its own, so for a bracketed host with no port (http://[::1]/v1) that cut the
host down to "[:" and matched nothing — and the bare "::1" arm was dead for the
same reason. Only the ported spelling worked, which is the one the test covered.

is_free_call therefore returned false for a local server, and since a local
model name is not in RATES, estimate_cost billed it at the DEFAULT_RATE
$3/$15-per-1M fallback: a fabricated cost for a free call.

Strip the port structurally instead — up to and including ] for a bracketed
host, whole for a bare IPv6, single-colon split otherwise — and extend the
existing test with the port-less forms.

fix(autopilot): honour a cancel on the scrape error path (#778)

The engine reports an early Stop as Err("scrape cancelled") — deliberately, per
its F6 comment: Err only when the user cancelled AND no items were recovered.
The command's Err arm had no cancel check, so it unconditionally called
fail_run_without_summaries + job_fail and returned {error}. The only cancel
handler sat on the Ok path and was never reached.

Two results: the UI showed Failed for a run the user deliberately stopped, and
because outcome_failed treats any {error} payload as a failure, the scheduler
re-ran the very scrape that was stopped.

Check cancel_token.is_cancelled() first in the Err arm and mirror the Ok-path
handler, returning {cancelled: true} — the shape outcome_failed already treats
as "not a failure".

Co-authored-by: Saeed Kolivand <51081940+saeedkolivand@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(postings): write interactions atomically and stop swallowing write errors (#776)

save() used fs::write, which truncates the target in place, and then discarded
the Result with .ok(). So a crash mid-write left interactions.json truncated,
and a failed write (read-only volume, full disk) was reported as success by
upsert/import_bundle while the record lived only in memory and vanished on the
next restart. A serialization failure was swallowed the same way.

Write to interactions.json.tmp and rename it over the target — the same trick
the corrupt-file backup path already uses — and log every failure, cleaning up
the temp file and leaving the previous file intact.

save() is called from upsert/clear_all/import_bundle, none of which return a
Result, so this logs rather than changing those public signatures.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(documents): validate an import bundle before clearing the store (#774)

import() called clear_all() — which wipes documents, vectors, posting_vectors
and match_scores — and only then deserialized the rows one by one, with no
enclosing transaction. A malformed row at index k therefore returned Err with
the user's originals already destroyed and rows 0..k half-inserted.

Deserialize every DocumentRecord and its optional vector first, then clear and
repopulate, matching ai_generations/applications/dedup/discovered, which all
validate up-front for this exact reason.

The vector + vectorSpace reconstruction moves to parse_exported_vector() with
unchanged behaviour, including the legacy Ollama/nomic-embed-text fallback.

Adds a regression test; it fails on the old code with "the prior library must
survive a failed import: left 0, right 1".

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(applications): make update_fields read-modify-write atomic (#772)

* fix(applications): make update_fields read-modify-write atomic

update_fields called self.get(id) — which takes and RELEASES the mutex — then
self.write_row(&app), which retakes it. write_row_conn is a full-column upsert,
so it re-persisted every column from the stale snapshot and silently clobbered
any writer that committed in the gap: the extension's answers.save/merge_answers
or a concurrent set_status.

Fold the lookup and the write into one transaction via row_by_id_conn, mirroring
upsert_internal, which already carries this exact fix.

The new race test fails on every run against the old structure ("merge_answers
entry N was lost to a concurrent update_fields") and passed 5/5 with the fix.

* fix(applications): hold one lock instead of opening a transaction

CI caught two problems with the first version of this fix.

R8 (no oversized modules) failed: applications/mod.rs is 1398 LOC upstream
against a 1400 hard cap, and the transaction version pushed it to 1407.

Clippy -D warnings failed too: replacing the self.write_row(&app) call left
write_row itself dead.

Both point the same way — the transaction was more machinery than the fix
needs. update_fields does ONE read and ONE write, and every writer shares a
single Connection behind this mutex, so holding the lock across both is what
actually prevents the lost update; the transaction only added atomicity that a
single upsert already has.

Take the lock once, use the conn-scoped row_by_id_conn/write_row_conn, and drop
the now-unused write_row. Net effect on the file is -3 LOC (1395), so it no
longer sits against the cap.

Verified: race test passes 5/5 with the fix and fails against upstream's
update_fields; cargo test --lib 2690 passed; architecture 11 passed; clippy
--all-targets --all-features -D warnings clean; fmt clean.

fix(scraping): read the remoteok id per json variant (#770)

The id arrives as a JSON number for some rows and a JSON string for others, so
it is typed Option<serde_json::Value>. Value::to_string() JSON-serialises,
which means Value::String("1234567") became "\"1234567\"" — quotes included —
and those quotes ended up inside posting.id (remoteok:"1234567") and
external_id, corrupting the stable PostingsCache key used for dedup and
interaction tracking. Only the Number variant serialised cleanly.

Extract normalize_id() and read the value per variant. Non-string/non-integer
values still map to an empty string, so the caller's is_empty() guard drops the
row unchanged.

The pre-existing tests only exercised a numeric id, which is exactly the
variant that escapes the bug.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(scraping): advertise only gzip on the linkedin client (#768)

The client sent `Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br`, but reqwest is built with
default-features = false and no gzip/brotli/deflate feature, so it never
auto-decompresses — and get_html decodes only the gzip magic bytes by hand.
A br/deflate response therefore reached String::from_utf8 as compressed bytes
and returned AppError::Parse("response was not valid UTF-8"), which propagates
out of search_guest page 0 and empties the whole LinkedIn board.

Advertise only gzip, and add a test pinning the header to what the decoder can
actually handle.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Saeed Kolivand <51081940+saeedkolivand@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(extraction): stop rtf hex escape from slicing a str mid-character (#766)

rtf_to_text decoded \'xx via &rtf[i + 2..i + 4]. `rtf` is a Cow<str> and `i` is
a BYTE index, so that slice panics unless both ends land on UTF-8 char
boundaries — and the only guard was a length check (i + 3 < n). An RTF with
\' followed by a multi-byte character (e.g. \'€) therefore panicked with
"byte index N is not a char boundary", aborting extraction of the résumé.

Read the two digits from the `bytes` slice already in scope, validating them
as ASCII hex first. A malformed escape now advances past just the \' so the
following character's bytes stay intact; the truncated-at-end branch is
unchanged.

Adds regression tests for the multi-byte, truncated and well-formed cases.

Co-authored-by: thejesh23 <thejesh23@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Saeed Kolivand <51081940+saeedkolivand@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(extraction): do not emit bare w:p docx paragraphs twice (#764)

* fix(extraction): do not emit bare w:p docx paragraphs twice

parse_document chained xml.split("<w:p ") with xml.split("<w:p>"). For a
document that uses the bare <w:p> spelling (Google Docs, LibreOffice,
python-docx) the first split matches nothing and yields the WHOLE document as
one element — which parse_paragraph mines into a single boundary-less blob —
while the chained second split also yields every paragraph. All text was
emitted twice: once mashed together, once per paragraph. MS Word escaped it
only because its w:rsidR attributes leave the <w:p>-split branch empty.

Scan for the <w:p tag (requiring ' ' or '>' after it, so <w:pPr>/<w:pict> are
not mistaken for paragraphs) instead of splitting on one spelling; documents
mixing both spellings are now handled too.

Adds four regression tests that assert the EXACT extracted text — the existing
coverage used contains(), which stays green through duplication.

* test(extraction): pin self-closing w:p paragraph handling

Review follow-up on #764.

A self-closing <w:p/> is not treated as a paragraph start, so it folds into the
previous slice. That is harmless — it carries no runs, so it contributes no
text, and the old chained splits ignored it too — but the boundary was
undocumented and untested. Pin it with a test and note it in the doc comment.

Also inlines the single as_bytes() read, dropping the local binding.

* feat(scraping): cluster cross-board duplicates with pair tombstones

Recompute-at-ingest cross-board job clustering per ADR-029. One pure module
(scraping/cluster) blocks by normalized company + title first-token, then joins
by cosine (cached same-space vectors) or trigram-Jaccard of normalized titles;
a user "not a duplicate" pair tombstone against any member vetoes the join.

Surfaces:
- scraping/cluster/{normalize,mod}.rs: fold/company/title normalization, agency
  flag, trigram-Jaccard, assign_clusters, new_cluster_count (+ heavy unit tests).
- vector.rs (L0): hoisted cosine, re-exported from commands::ai_provider so the
  L1 cluster module reuses it without an upward import (arch R7).
- dedup/mod.rs: DedupStore on dedup.db (pair tombstones, DataStore + Resettable).
- commands/scrape.rs::recluster_postings_cache wired after both scrape paths;
  postings::apply_cluster_annotations patches cache items by id.
- autopilot: FoundJob cluster fields; record_run clusters the merged list and
  returns the all-new-cluster count; cluster-aware minMatchScore retention.
- IPC: dedup_mark_not_duplicate (5-file flow, gen:ipc) + JobPreferences
  extraAgencyCompanies single-column setter; tauri-client + service hooks.

feat(extension): match autofill fields labeled in major eu languages (#738)

* feat(extension): match autofill fields labeled in major eu languages

Tier-2 free-text field matching (matchNamedKey) only understood English
labels, so German/French/Spanish/Italian/Polish/... application forms
(Vorname, Prénom, Nombre, Correo electrónico, Telefonnummer, Wohnort, ...)
never autofilled. Tier-1 autocomplete matching was already language-agnostic.

- Refactor matchNamedKey into an ordered, first-match-wins {key, pattern}[]
  table and widen every pattern to DE/FR/ES/IT/NL/PL/PT/SV/DA (+ NO/FI where
  the words coincide). Combined full-name phrases (nombre completo, imie i
  nazwisko, ...) run before first/last so they resolve to fullName, not just a
  first token. name/nombre/nome carry negative lookaheads so username/company
  fields (Nombre de usuario / Nombre de la empresa / Nome utente) never fill.
- Strip diacritics (NFD + \p{Diacritic}) inside textSignal so the signal is
  accent-free and all keywords can be written accent-free. matchExtraLink is
  unchanged (its normalizeLabel double-strip is idempotent). field-signal.ts
  keeps ZERO imports (it is inlined into the classic fill.js/capture.js).
- Localize the generic-name denylist (schule/universitat/firma/usuario/...) and
  the AMBIGUOUS sensitive-PII denylist (geburtsdatum, dni, pesel, codice
  fiscale, personnummer, ...) so school/company fields and national-id/DOB
  fields are never filled or captured.

Per-language tests (DE/FR/ES/IT/PL) cover positive fills incl. accented labels
and negative school/company/username guards. Full extension suite: 499 pass.

fix(export): keep Unicode letters in exported filenames (#729)

* fix(export): keep unicode letters in exported filenames

The filename sanitiser used /[^a-zA-Z0-9\s-]/g, which deleted every
non-ASCII letter. For users whose name contained Cyrillic, CJK, Arabic,
Greek, Hebrew, Thai or any other non-Latin script, sanitize() returned
an empty string and the exported file was named
Candidate-Role-Company-<type>.<ext>, dropping the user's name entirely.

Switch to the Unicode-aware class /[^\p{L}\p{N}\s-]/gu so all Unicode
letters/numbers survive. Modern filesystems (APFS, NTFS, ext4) all
accept Unicode filenames. The existing NFD + combining-mark strip runs
first, so Latin-with-diacritics behaviour (Jöhn -> John) is unchanged.

* style: format export sanitize chain with prettier

fix(links): capture markdown urls containing balanced parens (#733)

MD_LINK_SPAN_RE used `[^)]+` for the URL group, which stops at the
first `)`. A markdown link to a Wikipedia disambiguation URL like
`[Python](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language))`
was truncated to `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming`,
producing a broken suggestion in the editor's link pick-list.

Allow one level of balanced parens inside the URL group, with the
existing 200/2000-char bounds preserved so the regex stays linear on
adversarial input.

fix(analyze): drop false language mismatch when a language is empty string (#731)

validateAndRepair coerced an empty-string language field to 'unknown'
for the output but then decided mismatch on the ORIGINAL raw value.
An empty string is a string, and "" !== 'unknown', so a model reply
like { resume: "", jobAd: "fr" } produced { resume: 'unknown', jobAd:
'fr', mismatch: true } — self-contradictory and lit up the amber
mismatch banner alongside a resume language shown as "unknown".

Compute mismatch on the coerced values so the guard the comment
describes actually applies.

fix(language-detection): return 'unknown' for codes outside the map (#727)

detectLanguage()'s docstring promises to return an ISO 639-1 code, but
LANGUAGE_MAP[code] || code leaks franc's raw ISO 639-3 code (e.g. ukr,
bul, ben, pes) whenever the detected language is not in the map.
Downstream getLanguageName() then can't resolve it and displays the raw
3-letter code to the user.

Fall back to 'unknown' so the ISO 639-1 contract holds.

fix(referral): pass iso code instead of display name to locale (#725)

The referral draft hook was passing detectLanguages(...).resumeName
("English", "Deutsch", "Français") into locale, which is fed through
safeLocale(). safeLocale only recognises ISO 639-1 codes and collapses
anything else to 'en', so non-English users always received an English
referral draft.

Pass .resume (the ISO code) at both call sites.

docs: add the critic contract and seed the miss ledger (#716)

* docs: add the critic contract and seed the miss ledger

* build: add a docs/meta fast path to the pre-push gate

A push whose branch contribution (merge-base(origin/main)..HEAD) is entirely
docs/meta content — markdown, docs/**, the .claude agent/skill/command fleet,
CLAUDE.md, landing/*.html — now skips the build/typecheck/test + rust + AI-review
gates and runs only the cheap guards those paths need: agent-system drift when
.claude/** or CLAUDE.md changed, landing-diagram drift when landing/ changed, and
prettier --check on the changed files.

Basing the decision on the branch's own diff (not the raw remote_sha..local_sha
push range) means a re-push that merely folds a fresh origin/main in — exactly
what the hook's own stale-base sync forces on an open PR — still fast-paths a
docs-only PR instead of re-gating main's already-merged, already-tested code,
which is what dragged apps/desktop/src and packages/ into NODE=1 and ran the full
turbo+vitest gate on a docs-only PR. Any executable/config/build input, an
undetermined push range, an unresolvable merge-base, or PREPUSH_FULL=1 all still
take the full gate — uncertainty never fast-paths.
